content:
# Deuteronomy 23 — Disagreement Log

**Chapter:** Deuteronomy 23 (26 verses per MT) | **Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Agents compared:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA

---

## v.1 — Body language precision

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "wounded in the testicles" / "has his male member cut off" |
| LOGOS | "whose testicles are crushed" / "whose male organ is cut off" |
| PNEUMA | "emasculated by crushing or cutting" |

**Ruling:** LOGOS is the most anatomically precise while remaining formal. PNEUMA compresses both conditions into one phrase ("emasculated by crushing or cutting"), losing the distinction between the two Hebrew conditions. Consensus follows LOGOS.

---

## v.2 — "mamzer" rendering

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "No one of illegitimate birth [mamzer]" |
| LOGOS | "No one born of a forbidden union" |
| PNEUMA | "No one born of a forbidden union" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *mamzer* = likely offspring of incest or adultery (not mere illegitimacy). EMET's "illegitimate birth" is too broad. LOGOS/PNEUMA: "born of a forbidden union" better reflects the scholarly consensus. Consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA.

---

## v.4 — "Aram-naharaim" vs. "Mesopotamia" vs. "Aram-Naharaim"

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "Pethor of Aram-naharaim" |
| LOGOS | "Pethor of Mesopotamia" |
| PNEUMA | "Pethor in Aram-Naharaim" |

**Ruling:** The Hebrew *Aram-naharaim* is the specific ancient name. LOGOS uses the Greco-Latin gloss "Mesopotamia." Project convention preserves Hebrew place names. Consensus follows EMET/PNEUMA: "Aram-naharaim."

---

## v.5 — "was not willing to listen" (EMET) vs. "would not listen" (LOGOS) vs. "refused to listen" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "the LORD your God was not willing to listen to Balaam" |
| LOGOS | "the LORD your God would not listen to Balaam" |
| PNEUMA | "the LORD your God refused to listen to Balaam" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *lo avah YHWH* = "the LORD was not willing/did not consent." EMET: "was not willing." LOGOS: "would not." PNEUMA: "refused." Consensus follows EMET ("was not willing") — most literal; "refused" (PNEUMA) implies struggle or prior request more strongly than MT.

---

## v.6 — "their peace or their prosperity" (EMET) vs. "their peace or their prosperity" (LOGOS) vs. "peace or good relations" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "their peace or their prosperity" |
| LOGOS | "their peace or their prosperity" |
| PNEUMA | "peace or good relations with them" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *shelomam ve-tovotam* = "their peace and their good/prosperity." EMET/LOGOS are identical. PNEUMA's "good relations" loses the *tov* nuance of prosperity/welfare. Consensus follows EMET/LOGOS.

---

## v.7 — "abhor" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "despise" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "You shall not abhor an Edomite" |
| LOGOS | "You shall not abhor an Edomite" |
| PNEUMA | "Do not despise an Edomite" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *lo-tetaev* = "you shall not abhor/loathe." EMET/LOGOS: "abhor." PNEUMA: "despise." "Abhor" (EMET/LOGOS) is the stronger and more accurate term. Consensus follows EMET/LOGOS.

---

## v.9 — "a company" (EMET) vs. "encamped" (LOGOS) vs. "encamped" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "When you go out as a company against your enemies" |
| LOGOS | "When you are encamped against your enemies" |
| PNEUMA | "When you are encamped against your enemies" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *ki tetse machaneh* = "when you go out [as/in] a camp." EMET: "as a company." LOGOS/PNEUMA: "when you are encamped." Consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA — "encamped against" is the precise military meaning.

---

## v.10 — "nocturnal occurrence [qeri lailah]" vs. "nocturnal emission" vs. "nocturnal emission"

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "because of a nocturnal occurrence [qeri lailah]" |
| LOGOS | "because of a nocturnal emission" |
| PNEUMA | "because of a nocturnal emission" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *mikreh laylaah* = "an occurrence of the night." EMET preserves the transliteration. LOGOS/PNEUMA: "nocturnal emission" is the standard rendering. Consensus follows LOGOS/PNEUMA.

---

## v.12 — "designated place" (EMET) vs. "a place" (LOGOS) vs. "designate a place" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "you shall have a designated place outside the camp" |
| LOGOS | "You shall have a place outside the camp" |
| PNEUMA | "Designate a place outside the camp where you can go to relieve yourself" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *yad tihyeh lekha* = "there shall be a place/hand for you." EMET: "designated place." LOGOS: "a place." PNEUMA adds "to relieve yourself" (explanation of purpose). Consensus follows LOGOS ("a place") — "designated" implies more formalization than MT warrants.

---

## v.13 — "peg" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "something to dig with" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "a peg [yated] with your equipment" |
| LOGOS | "a peg with your tools" |
| PNEUMA | "something to dig with" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *yated* = "peg/stake." EMET/LOGOS: "peg." PNEUMA: "something to dig with" — too vague. Consensus follows LOGOS ("peg").

---

## v.13 — "tools" (LOGOS) vs. "equipment" (EMET) vs. (not specified by PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "with your equipment" |
| LOGOS | "with your tools" |
| PNEUMA | "as part of your equipment" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *'al azenekha* = "upon/with your weapons/equipment." Military equipment. Consensus follows EMET ("equipment") over LOGOS's "tools."

---

## v.14 — "indecent thing [ervat davar]" (EMET) vs. "anything indecent" (LOGOS) vs. "anything impure" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "any indecent thing [ervat davar]" |
| LOGOS | "anything indecent" |
| PNEUMA | "anything impure" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *ervat davar* = "a matter of nakedness/indecency." EMET/LOGOS: "indecent." PNEUMA: "impure" (shifts the semantic domain from indecency to impurity). Consensus follows LOGOS ("any indecent thing").

---

## v.15 — "give back to his master" (EMET) vs. "give up to his master" (LOGOS) vs. "hand him over" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "You shall not give back to his master a slave who has escaped" |
| LOGOS | "You shall not give up to his master a slave who has escaped" |
| PNEUMA | "do not hand him over to his master" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *lo tasguir eved* = "you shall not hand over/return a slave." EMET: "give back." LOGOS: "give up." PNEUMA: "hand over." Consensus follows LOGOS ("give up") — more idiomatic for surrendering a fugitive.

---

## v.16 — "gates" (EMET) vs. "towns" (LOGOS) vs. "town" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "in one of your gates" |
| LOGOS | "within one of your towns" |
| PNEUMA | "in whatever town suits him best" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *be-achad sha'arekha* = "in one of your gates." "Gates" is the literal Hebrew idiom for "towns/settlements." Consensus follows LOGOS ("in one of your towns") as the standard rendering of this idiom in Deuteronomy.

---

## v.17 — "cult prostitute" (all three) — word choice for qedeshah/qadesh

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "cult prostitute [qedeshah]" / "cult prostitute [qadesh]" |
| LOGOS | "cult prostitute" (after note about convention) |
| PNEUMA | "temple prostitute" |

**Ruling:** EMET/LOGOS: "cult prostitute." PNEUMA: "temple prostitute." The Hebrew *qedeshah/qadesh* literally means "consecrated/holy woman/man." In this legal prohibitory context, the sexual-cultic function is operative. EMET and LOGOS both settle on "cult prostitute"; PNEUMA's "temple prostitute" is also accurate but "cult" better signals the Canaanite religious practice being prohibited. Consensus: "cult prostitute."

---

## v.18 — "fee of a prostitute" (EMET/LOGOS) vs. "earnings of a female prostitute" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "the fee of a prostitute [zonah]" |
| LOGOS | "the fee of a prostitute" |
| PNEUMA | "the earnings of a female prostitute" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *etnan zonah* = "fee/hire of a prostitute." EMET/LOGOS: "fee." PNEUMA: "earnings." PNEUMA also specifies "female" (because *zonah* is feminine). Consensus follows LOGOS ("fee of a prostitute").

---

## v.18 — "wages of a dog" — level of interpretive explanation

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "the wages of a dog" (no expansion) |
| LOGOS | "the wages of a dog" |
| PNEUMA | "the wages of a male prostitute" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *etnan kelev* = "fee of a dog." EMET/LOGOS preserve "dog" as the literal term (its reference to the *qadesh* is conveyed by the parallel with v.17). PNEUMA: "male prostitute" — interpretive rendering. Consensus follows EMET/LOGOS ("wages of a dog") — the text is self-interpreting via the v.17 parallel.

---

## v.19 — "charge interest [neshek]" (EMET) vs. "charge interest on loans" (LOGOS) vs. "charge interest" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "charge interest [neshek] to your brother" |
| LOGOS | "charge interest on loans to your brother" |
| PNEUMA | "charge a fellow Israelite interest" |

**Ruling:** LOGOS adds "on loans" (not in MT; implied). PNEUMA: "fellow Israelite" for "brother." Consensus: "charge interest to your brother" (EMET without bracket) — closest to MT.

---

## v.21 — "will surely require it of you" (EMET) vs. "will certainly demand it" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "the LORD your God will surely require it of you" |
| LOGOS | "the LORD your God will surely require it of you" |
| PNEUMA | "the LORD your God will certainly demand it of you" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *darsho yidreshennu* = cognate construction ("seek/require he will require it"). EMET/LOGOS: "surely require." PNEUMA: "certainly demand." Consensus follows EMET/LOGOS.

---

## v.24 — "your fill" (LOGOS) vs. "as many as you please, until you are full" (EMET) vs. "all the grapes you want" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "as many as you please [according to your soul's desire], until you are full" |
| LOGOS | "your fill of grapes, as many as you wish" |
| PNEUMA | "all the grapes you want until you are satisfied" |

**Ruling:** EMET's bracket "[according to your soul's desire]" adds the Hebrew *ke-nafshekha* explanatorily. LOGOS: "your fill... as many as you wish." PNEUMA: "all the grapes you want." Consensus follows LOGOS (removing the bracket): "your fill of grapes, as many as you wish, until you are satisfied."

---

## v.24 — "bag" (LOGOS) vs. "vessel" (EMET) vs. "basket" (PNEUMA)

| Agent | Rendering |
|-------|-----------|
| EMET | "shall not put any in your vessel" |
| LOGOS | "you shall not put any in your bag" |
| PNEUMA | "do not put any in your basket" |

**Ruling:** Hebrew *keli* = "vessel/container." All three differ. "Bag" (LOGOS) is the most natural modern equivalent for a portable container. Consensus follows LOGOS.

---

*SOPHIA — Deuteronomy 23 disagreements logged: 21*
